name: Grappling, Boarding, and Naval Melee
description: Within 1" ships may grapple, 20% per try, three tries a turn (and as many cuts, also 20%), one man to a grapple; grappled ships lie dead in the water and may be boarded — one man per turn per three feet of parallel deck, swimmers climbing aboard fighting beneath their foe. Melee is CHAINMAIL man-to-man on deck plans scaled to the figures. Command control reaches the leader's Charisma in inches (two lieutenants at one inch less; halved in melee; +1" per 10' of height; meleed men obey on 1-4 checked each turn); the unled do not respond — monsters always do. Forced back without room: overboard, off the deck, or surrender; falls save one-in-six per level fallen, damage a die per level (per two levels into water). Breaking doors, chopping decks, cutting rigging scale by strength — a giant tears what costs ten men a turn. A ship is captured when its crew's morale breaks or all defenders are dead or overboard.
